title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is used to find the length of a side in a right triangle. It states that the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides.

title: Identify the Known Values
text: In this problem, you are given the hypotenuse (15 meters) and one leg (9 meters). You need to find the length of the other leg.

title: Set Up the Equation
text: Using the Pythagorean Theorem, set up the equation: $$a^2+b^2={15}^2$$. Substitute the known values: $$9^2+b^2={15}^2$$.

title: Simplify the Equation
text: Calculate the squares of the known values: $$9^2$$ and $${15}^2$$.

title: Solve for the Unknown Side
text: Subtract $$81$$ from both sides of the equation to isolate $$b^2$$.

title: Find the Length of the Other Leg
text: Take the square root of both sides to find the length of the other leg.
